extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remco attacked solo 17km out on final categorized climb after neutralized stage honored deceased MÄDER Gino, won by nearly a minute.
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL attacked at 17km on final climb while rivals still emotionally compromised, exploiting unique neutralized race dynamics.
INEOS placed multiple riders in late move despite mourning context, revealing organizational autopilot even in neutralized scenarios.
VAN AERT bridged late but didn't commit to chase, suggesting emotional hesitation trumped tactical calculation in unusual circumstances.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: separated immediately from chasers including committed VAN AERT, showing superior climbing form despite shortened preparation
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: positive: initiated late racing after neutralization ended, demonstrating tactical awareness of symbolic moment to honor racing
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: neutral: followed moves and took podium sprint but didn't organize chase, unclear if tactical choice or emotional restraint
rider_signals.COQUARD Bryan: positive: third place suggests improved climbing form to survive categorized ascent and contest sprint
rider_signals.SKJELMOSE Mattias: neutral: held 8-second GC lead into final TT, vulnerable margin suggests time trialing weakness relative to GALL
